data.title: Vor Biopharma draws downgrades on strategic review
data.description:
Shares of Vor Biopharma (NASDAQ:VOR) continued to tumble on Friday as Baird and Stifel downgraded the gene therapy developer in reaction to its plans to explore strategic alternatives, which led to over a 70% decline in its stock price in the previous session.

Baird analyst Jack Allen downgraded the biotech to Neutral from Outperform, noting that the company is concurrently winding down its clinical and manufacturing operations, including ongoing clinical programs, and implementing a 95% reduction of staff.

The analyst slashed its price target to $0.25 from $14.00 per share based on his projection for the year-end cash position at the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based firm.

Meanwhile, Stifel’s Stephen Willey cut VOR’s rating to Hold from Buy and slashed its target price to $0.30 from $5.00 per share, noting that the company’s efforts to seek strategic alternatives as approved by its board are aimed at maximizing near- and longer-term shareholder value.

data.title: U.S. stocks mixed ahead of possible weekend China trade deal
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages were mixed in Friday's afternoon trade, a day after they rallied following a U.S.-U.K. trade agreement that renewed optimism over more potential economic and trade partnerships.

Both the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) were unchanged, while the blue-chip Dow Jones (DJI) was last -0.2%.

Over the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields inched lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.37%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.88%.

Markets did not advance on Friday, despite closing higher on Thursday, after President Donald Trump announced the trade agreement with the United Kingdom. 

“U.S. consumers are worse off than in January, though better off than a week ago. Some U.K. exporters get better conditions, some U.S. exporters might get better conditions. Yesterday’s Bank of England's rate cut was a more important economic event,” UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Probably more important for markets than the UK “deal” itself were Trump’s comments on China, saying that he expects “substantive” talks that could then yield tariff cuts, Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Elsewhere on the tariff front, as Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and other U.S. officials head to Switzerland to start talks with high-level Chinese officials on trade, President Donald Trump suggested that reducing the current 145% tariff to 80% "seems right."

"Many Trade Deals in the hopper, all good (GREAT!) ones!" he posted on Truth Social, Friday morning.

Furthermore, among Friday's top gainers are Insulet (PODD), 20.5%; Microchip Technology (MCHP), 11.2%; and First Solar (FSLR), 7.2%. 

Top decliners include Akamai Technologies (AKAM), -9.2%; Expedia (EXPE), -7.2%; and Crowdstrike Holdings (CRWD), -4.2%, after U.S. prosecutors and regulators probed a $32M deal between the company and Carahsoft.

data.title: Metallus projects $30M revenue from aerospace and defense initiatives in 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Metallus Inc. (MTUS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Mike Williams highlighted growing demand for domestic steel, citing a 50% year-over-year increase in order backlog. He emphasized recent market share gains and ongoing focus on cost management. The company supports the expansion of steel tariffs, viewing them as beneficial for domestic production competitiveness.
  • Shipments increased 17% compared to the prior quarter, driven by higher industrial, automotive, and energy shipments, although aerospace and defense shipments were lower due to customer start-up challenges.
  • Investments in safety management systems continue, with $5 million allocated for 2025. Past safety innovations have shown significant improvements, including an 80% reduction in exposure hazards through automation.
  • CFO Kris Westbrooks reported Q1 net sales of $280.5 million, a sequential increase of $40 million, attributed to higher shipments across most end markets. Adjusted EBITDA rose to $17.7 million, supported by lower manufacturing costs and improved cost absorption.
  • The net income for Q1 was $1.3 million ($0.03 per diluted share), with adjusted net income of $3.2 million ($0.07 per diluted share).

Outlook

  • Management expects Q2 adjusted EBITDA to increase sequentially, supported by higher aerospace and defense shipments and lead times extending to July for bar and tube products.
  • Spot price increases on SBQ and seamless mechanical tubing products were implemented, with price hikes of $60 to $120 per ton for SBQ and $100 per ton for tubing products starting July.
  • Melt utilization is projected to improve further in Q2, fueling operational performance and cost management.
  • Aerospace and defense revenues are on track to reach $30 million in 2025 from Vacuum Arc Remelt steel and related products, while new military applications are expected to ramp up in H2 2025.

Financial Results

  • Q1 shipments totaled 22,700 tons, with a sequential increase in industrial shipments by 33%, energy by 24%, and automotive by 9%.
  • Manufacturing costs decreased by $12.5 million sequentially, driven by higher production volumes and reduced maintenance costs.
  • Capital expenditures for Q1 were $27.5 million, including $14 million supported by government funding. The full-year CapEx forecast remains at $125 million, with $90 million funded by U.S. government programs.
  • The company reported a $38.9 million operating cash outflow during Q1, primarily due to significant pension contributions. Total cash and equivalents stood at $180.3 million.
  • Pension contributions amounted to $52.6 million in Q1, with further reductions in required contributions expected in future years.

Q&A

  • Samuel McKinney, KeyBanc Capital Markets: Questioned if Q1 shipment growth was linked to tariff-related pull-forward demand. CEO Williams clarified that market share gains and distributor restocking were the primary drivers, with minimal tariff-related impact.
  • McKinney also asked about energy market trends. Williams noted increasing domestic sourcing by energy customers, supported by the displacement of imports.
  • Dave Storms, Stonegate: Inquired about aerospace and defense customer challenges. Williams explained delays due to new facility commissioning but mentioned progress and new inquiries from international customers.
  • Storms asked about production capacity. Williams confirmed sufficient capacity to meet growing demand.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about macroeconomic uncertainties and customer-specific challenges in aerospace and defense. Their tone was neutral to slightly positive, reflecting cautious optimism about demand recovery.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, emphasizing strong order backlogs and strategic investments. Statements like “we are confident” and focus on cost management underscored their positive outlook.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts appeared more optimistic, while management showed consistent confidence in execution and growth initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to emphasize higher Q2 EBITDA, driven by increased aerospace and defense shipments and spot price improvements.
  • Strategic focus expanded within aerospace and defense, targeting $30 million in revenues from new initiatives.
  • Management tone remained confident, while analysts showed increased optimism compared to Q4 2024 when demand uncertainties were higher.
  • Key metrics improved significantly, including a 17% increase in shipments and higher adjusted EBITDA.

Risks and Concerns

  • Macroeconomic uncertainties and potential delays in trade policy implementation were noted as external challenges.
  • Analysts raised concerns about aerospace and defense customer start-up delays. Management assured progress and highlighted new customer inquiries.
  • Energy markets remain vulnerable to import dynamics despite improving domestic sourcing trends.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized strong momentum in demand, especially in industrial, automotive, and energy segments, bolstered by strategic price increases and operational improvements. Aerospace and defense remain a key growth driver, with $30 million in revenue targeted for 2025. Investments in safety, cost control, and government-funded projects further strengthen the company’s position. Challenges in macroeconomics and specific customer delays were acknowledged but mitigated by a strong balance sheet and strategic initiatives.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA analyst downgrades: VTRS, MRVL, MAR, NTR, CRSP, DB, VRSK, OCSL
data.description:
Here are the latest downgrades by Seeking Alpha analysts:

Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS). Buy to sell by Edmund Ingham. "Despite some promising new products, the company's ability to grow while managing its debt and satisfying Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS) shareholders remains questionable, making selling a prudent option."

Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL). Buy to hold by Uttam Dey. "Downgrading Marvell (NASDAQ:MRVL) to a hold due to indefinite postponement of Investor Day, suggesting lost custom silicon design wins and increased competition."

Marriott International (NASDAQ:MAR). Buy to hold by Daniel Javier. "Marriott (NASDAQ:MAR) remains a solid hotel stock in my watchlist, much better than Hilton (HLT) and Hyatt (H)...Yet, the current price is still quite high to make a position. Technicals also show some risks, so lightening up MAR’s shares can be wise to minimize risks without removing it completely."

Nutrien (NYSE:NTR). Buy to hold by Vladimir Dimitrov. "Lower net selling prices in conjunction with cost pressures are likely to continue to put pressure on margins in the coming months. Based on current expectations about the rest of 2025, the recent share price rally appears to have been premature."

CRISPR Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CRSP). Hold to sell by Stephen Ayers."In light of Casgevy’s slow marketization, persistent net losses, and regulatory headwinds, CRISPR (NASDAQ:CRSP) is becoming increasingly difficult to hold. To be blunt, CRISPR’s (CRSP) cash position and strategic collaboration with Vertex (VRTX) are its only 'saving grace' at this moment."

Deutsche Bank (NYSE:DB). Buy to hold by Pearl Gray Equity and Research. "Despite Deutsche's (NYSE:DB) ongoing strengths, systematic risk is heating up, posing threats to the bank's net interest income and investment banking advisory services. In addition, Deutsche (DB) is trading above its five-year price-to-book median and arguably possesses a compressed dividend yield."

Oaktree Specialty Lending (NASDAQ:OCSL). Buy to sell by The Dividend Collectuh. "Although Oaktree Specialty Lending's (NASDAQ:OCSL) recent dividend cut may have freed up capital for them to invest back into the company, their 13.2% yield is still highly questionable as non-accruals have continued to rise."

Verisk Analytics (NASDAQ:VRSK). Buy to hold by Redfox Capital. "Despite solid execution and strategic initiatives, Verisk's (NASDAQ:VRSK) current valuation fully reflects these positives, suggesting limited upside and potential risk of multiple contractions."

data.title: Plains All American outlines $1.1B in adjusted free cash flow for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Plains All American Pipeline, L.P. (PAA) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Willie Chiang highlighted the company’s reported adjusted EBITDA of $754 million for Q1 2025. He discussed the market challenges, including trade tariff uncertainties and OPEC supply dynamics, which have led to volatile commodity prices. Despite this, management remains confident in the medium-to-long-term market outlook.
  • Chiang emphasized efficient growth initiatives, including the completion of a 30,000-barrel-per-day fractionation project at Fort Saskatchewan in Q2 and two acquisitions in the crude segment: the Cheyenne Pipeline and Black Knight Midstream.
  • The company reiterated its focus on capital discipline, maintaining a flexible balance sheet, and returning cash to unitholders. The leverage ratio remains at the low end of the target range.
  • CFO Al Swanson reported crude oil segment adjusted EBITDA of $559 million, impacted by weather-related refinery downtime, but noted recovery in volumes during April and May. NGL segment adjusted EBITDA reached $189 million, bolstered by higher NGL sales volumes and frac spreads.
  • Chiang announced the retirement of President and Co-Founder Harry Pefanis, thanking him for his decades of contributions to the company.

Outlook

  • Management maintained their 2025 guidance assumptions, including $75 per barrel WTI pricing and Permian growth of 200,000 to 300,000 barrels per day.
  • Plains expects adjusted free cash flow of approximately $1.1 billion for 2025, which excludes changes in assets and liabilities and is reduced by approximately $635 million for acquisitions.
  • CEO Chiang noted that the outlook for Permian production remains constructive and aligned with guidance ranges, despite recent market volatility.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2025 reached $754 million, driven by strong performance in the NGL and crude oil segments.
  • The company completed $55 million in bolt-on acquisitions, including Black Knight Midstream, and acquired the remaining 50% interest in the Cheyenne Pipeline.
  • Free cash flow for Q1 was robust, supporting the company’s ongoing capital allocation strategy.
  • Substantial hedging in the NGL segment, with 80% of estimated C3+ spec product sales protected for 2025, provided stability against commodity price fluctuations.

Q&A

  • Gabriel Moreen, Mizuho: Asked about capital allocation priorities amid volatility. CFO Swanson stated the focus remains on distribution growth as the primary method of returning cash to shareholders, with unit repurchases being opportunistic.
  • Michael Blum, Wells Fargo: Inquired about the M&A landscape. CEO Chiang responded that volatile markets may create opportunities, but capital discipline remains critical in evaluating acquisitions.
  • Manav Gupta, UBS: Requested details on recent acquisitions like Black Knight Midstream. COO Chris Chandler explained the deal’s strategic fit within the Permian Basin and its alignment with long-term operational goals.
  • Theresa Chen, Barclays: Asked about M&A opportunities related to market volatility. CEO Chiang emphasized that the company’s integrated system allows for synergies and strategic bolt-ons, which are evaluated on a case-by-case basis.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about market volatility and its potential impact on capital allocation and M&A strategy. They appeared neutral to cautiously optimistic in tone overall.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing resilience and strategic execution despite external uncertainties. However, responses during the Q&A highlighted a focus on cautious capital allocation and scenario planning.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence in navigating market volatility appeared consistent, while analysts seemed more focused on potential risks from macroeconomic factors.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EBITDA increased to $754 million in Q1 2025 from $729 million in Q4 2024, reflecting improved performance in the NGL segment.
  • The company reiterated its Permian crude production guidance of 200,000 to 300,000 barrels per day for 2025, consistent with previous quarters.
  • Capital allocation priorities remained unchanged, with management emphasizing efficient growth and disciplined acquisitions.
  • Analysts continued to focus on M&A and capital allocation, though there was an increased emphasis on the implications of market volatility.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted ongoing uncertainties related to trade tariffs and OPEC supply dynamics, which could impact commodity prices and market stability.
  • Analysts questioned the potential impact of market volatility on Plains’ ability to execute bolt-on acquisitions and maintain financial flexibility.
  • Management emphasized hedging strategies and a robust balance sheet as mitigation measures against these risks.

Final Takeaway

Plains All American Pipeline reported solid Q1 2025 performance, supported by strategic acquisitions, efficient growth initiatives, and a resilient NGL segment. Management emphasized a disciplined approach to capital allocation and a focus on returning cash to unitholders, while maintaining guidance amid a volatile market. The company remains well-positioned to navigate uncertainties and leverage its integrated asset base for long-term growth.
